questions.question: Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order. Russell Wilson, an NFL P. about Naomi Osaka saying that her Q. has been spectacular to watch R. humility and dedication to others S. player for the Seattle Seahawks, wrote
questions.explanation: The correct order of the parts to form a coherent sentence is: SPRQ The sentence reads: "Russell Wilson, an NFL player for the Seattle Seahawks, wrote about Naomi Osaka saying that her humility and dedication to others has been spectacular to watch."

questions.question: Complete the following collocation. When I got the job, I knew what _______ money really is.
questions.explanation: The correct collocation to complete the sentence is: hard-earned Hard-earned: Money that has been obtained through hard work, effort, or laborious means.

questions.question: Select the correct direct form of the given sentence. Shravan said that the prisoner had slept throughout the journey.
options: Shravan said, "The prisoner had been sleeping throughout the journey."
Shravan said, "The prisoner sleeps throughout the journey."
Shravan says, "The prisoner has slept throughout the journey."
Shravan said, "The prisoner slept throughout the journey."
questions.explanation: The correct answer is d: Shravan said, "The prisoner slept throughout the journey." Direct speech ---- Indirect speech Past perfect ---- past perfect continuous tense Therefore, the correct direct form of the given sentence is: Shravan said, "The prisoner slept throughout the journey."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. Marcella seemed m̲o̲r̲o̲s̲e̲ and downcast when she refused to have dinner.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate synonym of the underlined word "morose" in the given sentence is: Gloomy Morose: bad-tempered, and not saying much to other people. Gloomy: Dark or poorly lit, especially so as to appear depressing or frightening. Excited: Feeling or showing enthusiasm, eagerness, or anticipation. Jocund: Cheerful and lighthearted. Liberal: accepting different opinions or kinds of behaviour; tolerant

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. Health club membership has grown b̲y̲ ̲l̲e̲a̲p̲s̲ ̲a̲n̲d̲ ̲b̲o̲u̲n̲d̲s̲ after the pandemic.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment "by leaps and bounds" in the given sentence is: very quickly The idiom "by leaps and bounds" means to progress or advance rapidly or in large increments. It implies significant and noticeable progress or growth over a relatively short period of time.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate collocating word to fill in the blank. This statement of the political leader has created________confusion.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate collocating word to fill in the blank is: Utter Heavy: Having great weight; difficult to lift or move. Weighty: Having significance or importance. Vast: Extremely large in area, size, amount, or degree. Utter: Complete; absolute; total; without qualification or condition.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the given sentence. The efforts of the doctors came to a f̲u̲t̲i̲l̲e̲ end with the death of the patient.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is c: Fruitless Futile: Serving no useful purpose. Fruitless: Failing to achieve the desired result; unproductive; unsuccessful. Terrifying: Causing extreme fear or horror; extremely frightening. Startling: Surprising or shocking; causing sudden alarm or astonishment. Enriching: Making someone or something richer or more meaningful.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Omnivores get energy and nutrients from plant and animal materials by digesting carbs, protein, fat and fibre and metabolising the nutrients and energy of the sources ingested. B. The many diverse animals categorised as omnivores may be divided into sub-categories based on their dietary habits. C. An omnivore is an animal that can feed and thrive on both, plant and animal substances. D. They also have the capacity to integrate food sources like algae, fungus and bacteria into their diet.
questions.explanation: The correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph is: CADB • Sentence C introduces the topic by defining what an omnivore is. • Sentence A provides information about how omnivores obtain energy and nutrients. • Sentence D further elaborates on the diverse sources of food that omnivores can consume. • Sentence B concludes by mentioning the categorization of omnivores based on their dietary habits.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. The driver was taken to the nearest dispensary by the beggars.
options: To the nearest dispensary, the beggars took the driver.
The beggars take the driver to the nearest dispensary.
The beggars took the driver to the nearest dispensary.
The beggars took the driver to dispensary.
questions.explanation: The correct option that expresses the given sentence in active voice is: The beggars took the driver to the nearest dispensary. Active voice ---- Passive voice (Simple past tense) Subject + V2 + Object ---- Object + was/were + V3 + by + subject Therefore, the correct answer is: “The beggars took the driver to the nearest dispensary.”

questions.question: Select the sentences that contains no spelling errors.
questions.explanation: The sentence that contains no spelling errors is: Diabetes is a chronic disease.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The man who / was committed / the theft last night / was caught this evening.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is a: was committed To correct the sentence, the verb "was committed" should be replaced with a verb that indicates the action of committing the theft. A more appropriate phrasing would be "committed the theft" or "perpetrated the theft." Therefore, the corrected sentence would be: "The man who committed the theft last night was caught this evening."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. The poet managed to paint a v̲i̲v̲i̲d̲ picture of the landscape by the river through words.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is a: Evocative "Vivid" is an adjective that describes something that is very clear, intense, or detailed, often in terms of visual imagery. Evocative: Bringing strong images, memories, or feelings to mind; suggestive; stirring emotions or memories. Contemporary: Belonging to or occurring in the present; modern; current. Blurred: Not clear or distinct; lacking sharpness or definition. Metaphoric: Relating to or containing metaphors; figurative; symbolic.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution’. The students decided to surprise t̲h̲e̲i̲r̲ ̲t̲e̲a̲c̲h̲e̲r̲ ̲o̲n̲ ̲h̲e̲r̲ ̲b̲i̲r̲t̲h̲d̲a̲y̲.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is a: No substitution

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence. Roads in villages during rainy seasons become m̲u̲d̲d̲y̲.̲
questions.explanation: The most appropriate synonym for "muddy" in the sentence "Roads in villages during rainy seasons become muddy" is "filthy." • Muddy: Covered or filled with mud; dirty with mud. • Fuzzy: Having a frizzy, fluffy, or frayed texture or appearance; also used to describe something that is unclear or indistinct. • Unsoiled: Not dirty or stained; clean. • Filthy: Very dirty; covered with or characterized by filth. • Blurred: Not clear or distinct; smeared or smudged.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. I was fascinated by the novel.
options: The novel was fascinated to me.
questions.explanation: The option that expresses the sentence "I was fascinated by the novel" in the active voice is d: The novel fascinated me. Active voice ---- Passive voice Subject + V2 + Object ---- Object + was/were + V3 + by + Subject Therefore, the correct answer is: “The novel fascinated me.”

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. One of the students is always / late to the class; he has been warn / by the teacher many times; / but still, he keeps on being late.
options: but still, he keeps on being late
late to the class; he has been warn
One of the students is always
questions.explanation: The correct answer is b: late to the class; he has been warn The error is in the phrase "he has been warn." The correct past participle of the verb "warn" is "warned." So, it should be "he has been warned." Therefore, the corrected sentence would be: “One of the students is always late to the class; he has been warned by the teacher many times; but still, he keeps on being late.”

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. He w̲a̲s̲ ̲s̲i̲t̲t̲i̲n̲g̲ ̲b̲e̲s̲i̲d̲e̲s̲ ̲h̲i̲s̲ best friend during the event.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment "was sitting besides his" in the given sentence is c: was sitting beside his was sitting beside his: This option correctly uses "beside" to indicate "next to." It also retains the correct pronoun "his," maintaining the original meaning of the sentence. Thus, this is the correct choice.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word in the following sentence. There lies no guarantee that the hierchical structural framework between the elite and downtrodden sections of the society is right.
questions.explanation: The incorrectly spelt word in the given sentence is b: hierchical The correct spelling should be "hierarchical." • Downtrodden: Oppressed or treated badly by people in power. • Hierchical (incorrect spelling, should be "hierarchical"): Arranged in order of rank, status, or authority. • Structural: Related to or affecting the structure of something. • Guarantee: A formal promise or assurance that certain conditions will be fulfilled.

questions.question: Select the word segment that substitutes (replaces) the bracketed word segment correctly and completes the sentence meaningfully. The percentage of employees who called in sick and the number of employees who left their jobs within 2 years (were reflective of the level of job satisfaction).
options: were reflective on the level of job satisfaction
are reflective of the level of job satisfaction
has reflected the level of job satisfaction
is reflective of the level of job satisfaction
questions.explanation: The word segment that correctly substitutes (replaces) the bracketed word segment and completes the sentence meaningfully is ‘d’: is reflective of the level of job satisfaction This is because the subject "The percentage of employees who called in sick and the number of employees who left their jobs within 2 years" is considered a singular collective subject, and thus requires the singular verb "is."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Steer clear of
options: Avoid someone or something because it is dangerous for you
Show someone how to do a job or activity
questions.explanation: The most appropriate meaning of the idiom "Steer clear of" is: b) Avoid someone or something because it is dangerous for you This idiom means to stay away from someone or something because it could cause harm or trouble.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Comics have (1) ________ many superheroes. Bantul, the great was (2) ________ in the year 1965 in the backdrop of the Indo-Pak war. Indrajal comics was set up in 1964 and (3) __________ Aabid Surti, who gave birth to a character called Bhadur in 1976 wearing kurta and jeans. Raj comics too (4) ________ into this sphere with their (5) ________ toons Nagraj, Tiranga and Shakti together called Brahman Rakshak (protectors of the universe). Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1 would be: b) produced • Produced: Brought into existence; created or manufactured. • Fabricated: Made up or created, often with the implication of being false or invented. • Manufactured: Produced in large quantities using machinery; created or assembled in a systematic way. • Built: Constructed or assembled, typically referring to physical structures or objects.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Comics have (1) ________ many superheroes. Bantul, the great was (2) ________ in the year 1965 in the backdrop of the Indo-Pak war. Indrajal comics was set up in 1964 and (3) __________ Aabid Surti, who gave birth to a character called Bhadur in 1976 wearing kurta and jeans. Raj comics too (4) ________ into this sphere with their (5) ________ toons Nagraj, Tiranga and Shakti together called Brahman Rakshak (protectors of the universe). Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2 would be: d) created • Generated: Produced or created, often implying a process of creation or formation. • Promoted: Advanced or supported the growth or development of something. • Inspired: Encouraged or stimulated creative thinking or action; influenced by a specific idea or feeling. • Created: Brought into existence; made or formed something new.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Comics have (1) ________ many superheroes. Bantul, the great was (2) ________ in the year 1965 in the backdrop of the Indo-Pak war. Indrajal comics was set up in 1964 and (3) __________ Aabid Surti, who gave birth to a character called Bhadur in 1976 wearing kurta and jeans. Raj comics too (4) ________ into this sphere with their (5) ________ toons Nagraj, Tiranga and Shakti together called Brahman Rakshak (protectors of the universe). Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is b: employed When we say "Indrajal comics was set up in 1964 and employed Aabid Surti," it means that Indrajal comics hired or engaged Aabid Surti to work for them. In this context, "employed" suggests that Aabid Surti was hired by Indrajal comics, possibly as a writer or illustrator, and he created the character called Bhadur for them. • Contracted: Typically refers to entering into a formal agreement, often legally binding, between two parties for services or goods. • Employed: Refers to the action of hiring or engaging someone to work for an organization or company. • Authorized: Indicates giving official permission or approval for someone to do something. • Ordered: Can mean arranging for something to be made or supplied, typically by request or command.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Comics have (1) ________ many superheroes. Bantul, the great was (2) ________ in the year 1965 in the backdrop of the Indo-Pak war. Indrajal comics was set up in 1964 and (3) __________ Aabid Surti, who gave birth to a character called Bhadur in 1976 wearing kurta and jeans. Raj comics too (4) ________ into this sphere with their (5) ________ toons Nagraj, Tiranga and Shakti together called Brahman Rakshak (protectors of the universe). Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4 would be: b) entered The phrase "Raj comics too entered into this sphere" indicates that Raj comics also became involved or entered the realm of creating superheroes. In this context, "entered" is the most appropriate term to describe the action of Raj comics joining the domain of superhero comics. • Penetrated: Entered into or spread throughout something, often with the implication of force or thoroughness. • Entered: Went into or became involved in a particular sphere or area of activity. • Invaded: Entered forcefully or aggressively with the intent to take control or dominate. • Pierced: Made a hole or opening in something, often with a sharp object.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Comics have (1) ________ many superheroes. Bantul, the great was (2) ________ in the year 1965 in the backdrop of the Indo-Pak war. Indrajal comics was set up in 1964 and (3) __________ Aabid Surti, who gave birth to a character called Bhadur in 1976 wearing kurta and jeans. Raj comics too (4) ________ into this sphere with their (5) ________ toons Nagraj, Tiranga and Shakti together called Brahman Rakshak (protectors of the universe). Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5 would be: d) famous The word "famous" means well-known or widely recognized. In the context of the passage, it suggests that the toons (characters) created by Raj comics, namely Nagraj, Tiranga, and Shakti, are widely known or celebrated. Therefore, "famous" is the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
